match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Anamorphic film photography of SCARLETT in light casual beachwear suitable for water and promenade stepping into shallow ocean water at the shoreline of Bondi Beach during morning with a wave gently breaking at her feet creating splashes and foam around her ankles while she laughs with genuine immediate joy her body in mid-motion poised with natural energy the wet sand stretching out with subtle ripples and tide lines underfoot vast blue ocean horizon and distant surfers visible in the background golden sand tones meeting turquoise waves under bright breezy conditions captured eye-level wide on 35mm film stock with natural color grading crisp whites warm highlights and lifted airy shadows shallow depth of field emphasizing the foreground interaction with water while softly blurring the distant promenade and sky high-key natural sunlight with clean directional shadows and subtle lens flares evoking fresh sensory effortless cool energy

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: EXT. BONDI BEACH - Golden light beach with promenade
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
